text: Working with APIs allows you to connect different software applications and access data or services. To interact with an API, you typically send HTTP requests, such as GET to retrieve data or POST to submit information. The API usually responds with data in JSON format, which is easy for programs to read and process. Many APIs require authentication, often using an API key, to ensure only authorized users can access them. You also need to be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a certain time period. Finally, proper error handling is important; check response codes like 404 or 500 so your application can react gracefully when something goes wrong.
